skill.name: api-documentation-generator
skill.description: Generate comprehensive API documentation from code, including endpoints, parameters, examples, authentication, and error handling.
skill.summary: Creates developer-friendly API documentation from code and route details.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, reconnaissance, and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found these matches are documentation examples or plain prose in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, malicious network behavior, prompt injection, or credential exfiltration intent was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The scanner matched backticks used for markdown formatting, fenced examples, and inline endpoint paths. These examples are instructional documentation content and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched backticks occur inside markdown examples and prose. I found no script file, runtime hook, or instruction that executes those snippets.

low_findings.title: Network Calls Are API Documentation Samples
low_findings.description: The curl, fetch, requests, Postman schema, and resource URLs are examples used to show API documentation style. They do not send local data or secrets to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network indicators are embedded in sample documentation and reference links. There is no evidence of automatic network execution or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts are caused by ordinary documentation words, field descriptions, or API references. No MD5, DES, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are prose, examples, or resource names. I found no cryptographic code path or recommendation to use weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Sample Fields and Prose
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts occur in sample response fields, endpoint descriptions, and troubleshooting text. They do not instruct the agent to inspect the host system or collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are API documentation examples or user-facing guidance. No system discovery command or local host enumeration behavior is present.

content.value_statement: API teams often lose time turning routes, schemas, and examples into clear documentation. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce structured API references from code and implementation details.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes REST, GraphQL, and WebSocket API structure from provided code or descriptions.
Drafts endpoint documentation with methods, paths, parameters, bodies, responses, and authentication requirements.
Creates request examples for cURL, JavaScript or TypeScript, and Python.
Organizes OpenAPI, Swagger, and Postman-oriented documentation sections.
Documents errors, rate limits, pagination, filtering, sorting, and versioning conventions.
limitations: It does not verify live endpoint behavior unless tests or runtime access are provided.
It cannot infer hidden business rules that are not present in code, comments, or examples.
Generated examples need review before publishing to external developers.
It does not replace formal API security or compliance review.

use_cases.title: Document a New Backend API
use_cases.description: Create a complete endpoint reference while building or preparing a service for release.

use_cases.title: Refresh Outdated Developer Docs
use_cases.description: Compare current routes and schemas against existing docs, then rewrite stale sections.

use_cases.title: Prepare External Integration Guides
use_cases.description: Turn internal API behavior into onboarding material with examples, errors, and setup guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Document One Endpoint
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for a single route.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate clear documentation for this API endpoint. Include purpose, method, path, parameters, request body, responses, errors, and one request example.

prompt_templates.title: Create a REST API Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these route files and create a REST API reference. Group endpoints by resource and include authentication, pagination, filtering, errors, and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare OpenAPI-Ready Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task for structured API publishing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this API implementation into OpenAPI-ready documentation. Identify schemas, required fields, response models, status codes, and reusable components.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Rewrite Existing Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced maintenance task for existing documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these existing API docs with the current code. List mismatches, missing sections, outdated examples, and produce a corrected documentation draft.

output_examples.input: A user creation route with request validation and authentication middleware.
output_examples.output: A complete endpoint section with purpose, required bearer token, request fields, success response, validation errors, and client examples.

output_examples.input: A GraphQL query and resolver for fetching user profile data.
output_examples.output: A GraphQL documentation page covering query purpose, variables, returned fields, nested objects, and common error responses.

output_examples.input: An older API reference and updated route files.
output_examples.output: A concise change report plus revised documentation sections for renamed fields, new status codes, and missing pagination notes.

best_practices: Provide representative route files, schemas, middleware, and existing docs for better coverage.
Ask for separate sections for authentication, errors, pagination, and rate limits.
Review generated examples against a test or staging API before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated docs without checking endpoint behavior and example values.
Do not ask it to guess private business rules without source material.
Do not mix unrelated API versions in one generation request.

faq.question: Can this skill generate OpenAPI documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize endpoint details, schemas, responses, and reusable sections for OpenAPI or Swagger-style documentation.

faq.question: Does it execute API requests?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes documentation generation and examples. Live testing requires explicit tool access and user permission.

faq.question: Can it document GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for documenting queries, variables, returned fields, nested objects, and GraphQL errors.

faq.question: What input produces the best results?
faq.answer: Provide route files, controllers, schemas, middleware, authentication rules, and any current documentation.

faq.question: Can it update existing documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare current implementation details with existing docs and draft corrected sections.

faq.question: Is the generated documentation ready to publish?
faq.answer: It should be reviewed first. Teams should verify examples, status codes, and product-specific rules before release.
